metadata.source: CDC, NIMH, SAMHSA, American Psychological Association
metadata.description: Risk factors and causes of mental health conditions in adults
metadata.note: Based on peer-reviewed research and epidemiological studies

risk_factors.name: Work-Related Stress
risk_factors.description: Chronic stress from demanding work environment, job insecurity, workplace conflicts, or overwork. One of the most common mental health stressors for working adults.
risk_factors.prevalence: 34% of adults report high work stress
prevention_tips: Seek support from supervisors or HR

risk_factors.name: Financial Insecurity
risk_factors.description: Inadequate income, debt, job loss, or inability to meet basic needs. Financial stress is strongly linked to depression and anxiety across all age groups.
warning_signs: Avoidance of bills or financial statements

risk_factors.name: Relationship Problems
risk_factors.description: Conflict in romantic relationships, marriages, or significant relationships. Relationship quality significantly affects mental health outcomes in adults.
warning_signs: Emotional or physical distance from partner

risk_factors.name: Trauma and PTSD
risk_factors.description: Exposure to traumatic events (combat, assault, accidents, abuse). Can result in PTSD with significant impact on daily functioning and relationships.
resources: Crisis Text Line: Text HOME to 741741

risk_factors.name: Social Isolation
risk_factors.description: Lack of meaningful social connections or social support. Loneliness and isolation are increasingly common and strongly linked to depression in adults.
prevention_tips: Use technology to stay connected meaningfully

risk_factors.name: Unemployment or Job Loss
risk_factors.description: Loss of employment or inability to find work. Job loss triggers both financial stress and identity loss, major depression risk factors.
prevention_tips: Access unemployment benefits and career services
Volunteer to maintain sense of purpose
Consider retraining or career change programs

risk_factors.name: Chronic Medical Illness
risk_factors.description: Ongoing health conditions (diabetes, heart disease, cancer, etc.). Chronic illness significantly increases risk of depression and anxiety.
risk_factors.prevalence: 30% of chronic illness patients have depression
warning_signs: Treatment burden and medication side effects
prevention_tips: Integrate mental health with medical care
Address medication side effects with doctor

risk_factors.name: Family History of Mental Illness
risk_factors.description: Genetic predisposition to mental health conditions. Having family members with depression, anxiety, or addiction increases personal risk.
prevention_tips: Seek early intervention if symptoms appear

risk_factors.name: Substance Abuse or Dependence
risk_factors.description: Alcohol, drug, or medication misuse. Substance use often co-occurs with or masks mental health conditions.
risk_factors.prevalence: 10.1% of US adults have substance use disorder

risk_factors.name: Childhood Trauma or Abuse
risk_factors.description: History of childhood abuse, neglect, or trauma. Adverse childhood experiences significantly increase adult mental health risks.
prevention_tips: Process past experiences in safe environment

risk_factors.name: Caregiving Responsibilities
risk_factors.description: Stress from caring for aging parents, children, or disabled family members. Caregiver burden significantly increases mental health risks.
risk_factors.prevalence: 42 million caregivers in US, high burnout rates
prevention_tips: Take regular breaks and respite care

risk_factors.name: Life Transitions
risk_factors.description: Major life changes like retirement, empty nest, divorce, or relocation. Transitions challenge identity and stability.

risk_factors.name: Health Behavior Deficits
risk_factors.description: Poor sleep, sedentary lifestyle, poor nutrition, lack of exercise. These factors both contribute to and result from mental health problems.

risk_factors.name: Life Stressors and Major Events
risk_factors.description: Accumulation of life stressors or major life events. Multiple stressors together significantly increase mental health risks.
warning_signs: Inability to cope with normal tasks
prevention_tips: Seek social support and professional help
resources: Crisis Text Line: Text HOME to 741741

risk_factors.name: Discrimination and Stigma
risk_factors.description: Experience of discrimination based on race, gender, sexuality, or mental illness status. Increases stress and barriers to care.
prevention_tips: Build community with others with shared experiences
